steps.title: Focus on the distance from 9
steps.content: The original problem uses $|x-9|^2$ in $q(x)$. Since $q(x)$ depends on $x$ only through $|x-9|$, inputs that are the same distance from $9$ give the same output.

steps.title: Show that h and k are equal
steps.content:
For the two inputs:
$$|639-9|=630$$
$$|-621-9|=|-630|=630$$
Both inputs are $630$ units from $9$, so $q(639)=q(-621)$. Therefore, $h=k$.

steps.title: Use the zero exponents
steps.content:
The intended final expression is $9(-639)^{h-k}+621(9)^{k-h}$. Since $h=k$, both exponents are $0$:
$$h-k=0 \quad \text{and} \quad k-h=0$$

steps.title: Evaluate the expression
steps.content:
Any nonzero base raised to the $0$ power equals $1$, so:
$$9(-639)^0+621(9)^0=9+621=630$$
The answer is 630.
